user_message: Can you extract the competitive positioning from this escalation email for Coho Winery? I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-01-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:08:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:10:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:21:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-O trap (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-O failure mode (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
